page: Amazon Web Services r6in.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and the x86_64 architecture. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and a 3.5 GHz clock speed. The processor includes a 54 MB L3 cache and supports Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512). The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 RAM operating at 3 GHz, which provides 8.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage. The baseline network bandwidth is rated at 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across multiple categories. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is 1892.38, and the multi-core performance is 15467.9. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only large block speed of 95.46 GB/sec and a write large block speed of 56.42 GB/sec. In database and caching tests, the instance achieves 15.9 million operations per second for Redis SET operations and a PassMark database operations score of 7776.01. For language model tasks, the instance demonstrates strong performance in text generation using a small 135M model, reaching 272.59 tokens per second, while prompt processing for a medium 7B model operates at 31.94 tokens per second.

The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core makes this instance class qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require large memory footprints relative to compute capacity. A primary tradeoff is the complete absence of local NVMe storage, meaning all disk operations must traverse the network to EBS volumes. This configuration is appropriate for memory-bound applications, including in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise software deployments that do not require local scratch space.
description: Amazon Web Services r6in.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and a 3.5 GHz clock speed. Built on the x86_64 architecture and the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 128.0 GB of DDR4 RAM at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of memory per core. The instance includes a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth but does not feature local storage or GPUs. Benchmark results indicate solid performance, with a PassMark CPU score of 21082.67, 15.9 million Redis SET operations per second, and a strong text generation speed of 272.59 tokens/second on a small LLM. This hardware configuration is highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, making it appropriate for enterprise databases, in-memory caching, and data processing applications that require high memory density without the need for local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6in.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8375C CPU. It is built for memory-intensive workloads and offers 25 Gbps network bandwidth.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 instance featuring high-density DDR4 RAM and dedicated Intel Xeon processing for memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of DDR4 RAM providing 8.0 GB per core
Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 3.5 GHz clock speed and 54 MB L3 cache
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor optimization
Strong performance in small LLM text generation and Redis operations
Designed for in-memory databases, caching, and data-intensive applications
